title: jodina rhombifolia subsp. delasotae
intro: Jodina rhombifolia subsp. delasotae is a plant species, also known as the Argentine Jesuit's tea, which belongs to the family Anacardiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Jodina rhombifolia subsp. delasotae include the potential relief from pain and inflammation, as well as the treatment of skin conditions and infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, and it is also used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to utilize its antiseptic and antifungal properties.
